records.ety: [L. redarguere; pref. red-, re- re- + arguere to accuse, charge with: cf. F. rdarguer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Redargued (-g?d); p. pr. & vb. n. Redarguing.]

defs: To disprove; to refute; to confute; to reprove; to convict.
quotes: How shall I . . . suffer that God should redargue me at doomsday, and the angels reproach my lukewarmness?
Now this objection to the immediate cognition of external objects has, as far as I know, been redargued in three different ways.
